steps.title: Identify the subject and the parallel verb
steps.content: The subject is "the word 'stockade'" (singular). The sentence establishes parallelism with the coordinated clause "'cinch' ... derives from cincho," so the blank must match "derives" in tense and number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and parallelism, then eliminate
steps.content:
The blank needs a singular, simple-present verb that parallels "derives."

A. have derived — plural present perfect; wrong number and wrong tense. Eliminated.
B. were deriving — plural past progressive; wrong number and wrong tense. Eliminated.
C. derive — base form / plural present; wrong number ("stockade" is singular). Eliminated.
D. derives — third-person singular present; agrees with singular "stockade" and parallels "derives." Correct.

D is correct.
